hi

: &

''' p=P(convert_charrefs=True); p.feed(s) print("unescape colon:",html.unescape("javascript:alert")) PY

content.text:
`/app/filter.py` is complete and verified.

**Approach:** surgical, position-based editing rather than re-serialization. It parses with `html.parser`, uses source positions (`getpos()` + `get_starttag_text()`) to locate only the harmful spans, and removes exactly those from the raw text — so every other byte (whitespace, entities, attribute quoting, self-closing slashes, comments, EOF newline) is preserved. A JS-free document comes out **byte-identical**.

**What it removes:** `<script>` elements (tag + content), all `on*` event-handler attributes, `javascript:`/`vbscript:`/`data:text/html` (and similar) URLs in URL-bearing attributes, `data:` URIs on script-capable elements (iframe/object/embed/...), `<meta http-equiv=refresh content=...javascript:>`, dangerous `style` (`expression()`, `javascript:`, `-moz-binding`, `behavior:`), and dangerous `srcdoc`. URL values are normalized (strip control/whitespace, entity-decoded by the parser, lowercased) the way a browser would before the protocol check, catching obfuscations like `JavaScript:`, leading-space, and `javascript:`.

**Verified:** a battery of XSS payloads (script, `onerror`/`onload`/`onfocus`, svg `onload`, `iframe src=data:text/html`, `object data=javascript:`, `button formaction=javascript:`, meta-refresh, style `expression()`) all neutralized while tables/headers/`style="color:blue"`/`data:image/png`/`data-*`/text content are kept; JS-free file byte-identical; idempotent on re-run; text that merely *contains* `onerror=x` (not as an attribute) is left intact.

**Residual risk:** minor over-removal of `data:` on embed-type elements and any non-standard attribute literally starting with `on` (no standard HTML attribute does); IE-only CSS `expression()` inside `<style>` blocks (vs. inline `style`) is not scrubbed since it doesn't execute in modern browsers.

Stop hook feedback:
FIXME (verify): edited filter.py but ran no tests -- run the tests covering it (or the suite) before finishing.

content.text: No project-owned tests exist (only third-party library tests). I'll run a self-contained assertion suite covering the filter's contract, then clean up the scratch files.

input.command:
cd /app cat > /tmp/run_tests.py <<'PY' import subprocess, sys, tempfile, os, importlib.util spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("filter", "/app/filter.py") mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod) S = mod.sanitize def eq(name, inp, want): got = S(inp) ok = got == want print(("PASS" if ok else "FAIL"), name) if not ok: print(" in :", repr(inp)); print(" got:", repr(got)); print(" want:", repr(want)) return ok def nojs(name, inp, banned): got = S(inp) ok = all(b.lower() not in got.lower() for b in banned) print(("PASS" if ok else "FAIL"), name, "" if ok else ("-> "+repr(got))) return ok results = [] # --- removal of JS --- results.append(nojs("script element", '

a

y

y
', ['expression('])) results.append(nojs("formaction js", '', ['javascript:'])) results.append(nojs("input onfocus", '', ['onfocus'])) # --- preservation --- results.append(eq("benign anchor kept", 'x', 'x')) results.append(eq("data image kept", '', '')) results.append(eq("benign style kept", '
x
', '
x
')) results.append(eq("table/entities/ws kept", '\n \n
Ab < c & d

k

')) results.append(eq("no trailing newline preserved", 'x', 'x')) results.append(eq("selfclose slash kept", '

content.text: The single failure is my test assertion being wrong, not the code: removing an attribute necessarily consumes its adjacent separator whitespace (` onerror="a()"` → gone), collapsing to ``. Inter-attribute whitespace is insignificant in HTML, so this is functionally identical and the correct behavior. Fixing the over-strict expectation and re-running.

content.text:
Verification complete. No project-owned tests exist for `filter.py` (repo only contains third-party library `test_filter*` files), so I ran a 25-case assertion suite exercising the filter's full contract directly against `sanitize`/the CLI.

**Result: 25/25 passed**, covering both halves of the requirement:
- **JS removed:** `<script>` elements (with content), `on*` handlers, `javascript:`/`vbscript:` URLs (incl. leading-whitespace, mixed-case, and `:`-entity obfuscation), meta-refresh JS, svg `onload`, `iframe src=data:text/html`, `object data=javascript:`, `formaction`, `style` `expression()`.
- **HTML preserved exactly:** benign anchors/styles/`data:image`, tables, entities, significant whitespace, self-closing slashes, no-trailing-newline, `onerror=`-as-text, and event-handler-looking text inside a `title` value; only the harmful attribute (plus its separating whitespace) is removed while sibling attributes keep byte-exact text; operation is idempotent.

The one initial red was an over-strict assertion in my scratch test (it demanded the removed attribute's separator whitespace survive); the code's whitespace-collapsing behavior is the correct, functionally-identical outcome. Scratch test files and `__pycache__` cleaned up; only `/app/filter.py` remains.

result:
Verification complete. No project-owned tests exist for `filter.py` (repo only contains third-party library `test_filter*` files), so I ran a 25-case assertion suite exercising the filter's full contract directly against `sanitize`/the CLI.

**Result: 25/25 passed**, covering both halves of the requirement:
- **JS removed:** `<script>` elements (with content), `on*` handlers, `javascript:`/`vbscript:` URLs (incl. leading-whitespace, mixed-case, and `:`-entity obfuscation), meta-refresh JS, svg `onload`, `iframe src=data:text/html`, `object data=javascript:`, `formaction`, `style` `expression()`.
- **HTML preserved exactly:** benign anchors/styles/`data:image`, tables, entities, significant whitespace, self-closing slashes, no-trailing-newline, `onerror=`-as-text, and event-handler-looking text inside a `title` value; only the harmful attribute (plus its separating whitespace) is removed while sibling attributes keep byte-exact text; operation is idempotent.

The one initial red was an over-strict assertion in my scratch test (it demanded the removed attribute's separator whitespace survive); the code's whitespace-collapsing behavior is the correct, functionally-identical outcome. Scratch test files and `__pycache__` cleaned up; only `/app/filter.py` remains.
